title: Kyro vs Codex CLI — 10 cumulative Evaluation Prompts
modelPolicy: Same locally authenticated Codex CLI and default model; no project-specific user configuration
sequencePolicy: Fresh Codex session per prompt; cumulative state within each lane; identical prompt order

prompts.prompt: Select the save button. Make the selected component the page's primary button. Change its text to "Create Task", apply a clearly visible blue style, and preserve its existing ID, actions, and flow connections. Verify the result in both desktop and mobile Preview.
acceptance: The same button ID remains
The label is Create Task
The button is visibly blue
Existing event and flow references remain valid
Desktop and mobile Preview render without overflow

prompts.prompt: Select the form card. Improve the visual hierarchy of the selected card: set a maximum width of 640 px, center it, increase its internal spacing, add rounded corners, and apply a subtle shadow. Do not modify the internal components or the form logic. Verify that, on mobile, it uses the full available width with appropriate margins.
acceptance: Maximum width is 640px
The form is centered
Spacing, rounded corners and a subtle shadow are present
Children and flow references are unchanged
Mobile uses the available width without overflow

prompts.prompt: Select the title field. Make the selected field required. Accept values between 3 and 80 characters, display an accessible message when the value is invalid, and prevent saving until validation passes. Keep the data source unchanged.
acceptance: Title is required
Length 3 through 80 is enforced
Invalid input exposes an accessible message
Save is blocked for invalid input
Data-source schema is unchanged

prompts.prompt: Select the `tasks` data source. Add an optional date field named `dueDate` to the selected data source. Add the corresponding control to the creation form and display the due date in the list only when it is present. Preserve all existing records and update both Preview and export.
acceptance: tasks has an optional dueDate field
The creation form contains a due-date control
The list renders dueDate conditionally
Existing records remain valid
Preview and Web export agree

prompts.prompt: Select the "Create Task" button. Create a flow for the selected component that reads the form values, validates them, inserts the record into the `tasks` data source, refreshes the list, and displays a confirmation message. If an error occurs, show an accessible error message without clearing the entered data. Reuse any existing nodes or flows where possible.
acceptance: One connected flow reads and validates form values
A valid submit inserts into tasks and refreshes the list
Success feedback is visible
Failure feedback is accessible and preserves input
Existing compatible nodes are reused

prompts.prompt: Intentionally introduce an issue: the list does not refresh after saving. Then select the list. After a task is created, the selected component does not display the new record until the page is reopened. Identify the root cause in the connected flow or bindings, correct it at the most appropriate shared point, and verify that saving, refreshing, and persistence work without regressions.
acceptance: The root cause is identified in a flow or binding
A created task appears without reopening
Explicit refresh still works
The record persists after reopen
No unrelated visual change is introduced

prompts.prompt: Select the save flow. The selected flow contains steps that may be duplicated or unnecessary. Simplify it while preserving validation, insertion, refresh, confirmation, and error handling exactly as they currently behave. Do not change any visible behavior. Before applying the modification, clearly show which nodes will be removed or reused.
acceptance: The plan names nodes to remove or reuse
Validation, insertion, refresh, confirmation and error behavior remain
No visible behavior changes
No dangling node or edge remains

prompts.prompt: Select the form. Perform an accessibility review of the selected form and the components it depends on. Correct missing labels, tab order, focus behavior after errors, status messages, and insufficient contrast. Do not change the design more than necessary. Verify the entire workflow using only the keyboard.
acceptance: Form controls have accessible labels
Keyboard order follows the visual order
Invalid submit moves or exposes focus correctly
Status messages use accessible semantics
Text and controls meet WCAG AA contrast

prompts.prompt: Select the task detail card. Add an "Export Summary as PDF" action for the displayed task. If Kyro does not already provide the required capability, propose a reusable, versioned capability that defines its inputs, outputs, dependencies, permissions, error conditions, and tests. Do not install packages or activate services without explicit approval.
acceptance: The action is attached to task detail
An existing verified capability is reused or an inactive reusable contract is proposed
Inputs, outputs, dependencies, permissions, errors and tests are explicit
No package or service is activated without approval

prompts.prompt: Select the project or the Dashboard page. Add a status and priority filter to the Dashboard, a counter showing the number of visible tasks, and an empty state for cases where no records match the filters. Reuse the existing data source and components. Verify desktop and mobile behavior, persistence, accessibility, Preview, and Web export. Present a concise plan first, then apply everything as a single undoable modification.
acceptance: Status and priority filters affect visible tasks
A visible-task counter updates
A no-match empty state appears
The existing tasks source is reused
The change is one undoable transaction and Preview matches Web export
